title: salix phylicifolia var. laurina
intro: Salix phylicifolia var. laurina, also known as the laurel-leaved willow, is a variety of willow tree native to certain regions in Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Salix phylicifolia var. laurina include relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating various skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, often used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
